manifest.title: U.S.S. Serein: Black Current - Open World

ship.name: U.S.S. Serein
ship.openingCondition: Six weeks into a Vanta Wake recovery deployment. The ship is serviceable and well suited to escort, hazardous towing, and rapid response, but repeated mass-casualty transport cycles and tractor work have created technical debt. The incoming Executive Officer has been aboard for three days when Wreckfall Alpha erupts.

commandStructure.playerRole: Starfleet-designated regional recovery commander; Acting Captain after the opening emergence.

serviceHistory.commissioning: Entered service in the early 2370s as a medium-range escort and route-security vessel.
serviceHistory.wartimeService: Convoy escort, damaged-ship recovery, route clearance, and rapid reinforcement during the Dominion War.
serviceHistory.currentAssignment: Vanta Wake recovery command, hazardous salvage safety, survivor evacuation, evidence custody, and corridor reopening.
openProductionDecisions: Name and final action of the previous Executive Officer killed during the war

missionProfile: Protect life and freedom of navigation in the Vanta Wake.
Respond to emergences, stabilize wrecks, and coordinate survivor recovery.
Secure live ordnance and prevent militarized salvage.
Maintain lawful evidence custody without treating all rescue labor as criminal salvage.
Determine the physical cause of the Wake and establish a sustainable regional recovery regime.

keyCapabilities: Reinforced tractor-control suite and structural integrity support for damaged hulls
High-capacity transporters and modular cargo bays convertible to triage or quarantine use
Two heavy recovery shuttles and six workbees
Subspace wake-analysis sensor overlay tied to Ternion relay baselines
Tactical systems sufficient to secure a recovery zone and deter raiders
Expanded damage-control stores and portable containment equipment
knownTechnicalDebt: Uprated tractor emitters can overheat the original EPS trunks during sustained towing
Cargo-bay atmospheric partitions are worn after repeated emergency conversions
Transporter pattern buffers require extended cycling after mass-casualty operations
The wake-analysis overlay drifts near Blackmouth and during simultaneous anchor pulses
constraints: The Serein cannot tow more than one major hull safely while preserving rapid-response capability.
The ship lacks enough medical, quarantine, morgue, and housing capacity for a mass emergence.
Only two heavy recovery shuttles are available; losing one significantly reduces response reach.
High-power tractor or warp operations can increase local current pressure.
The Steamrunner-class tactical silhouette makes civilian salvagers and Cardassian families wary of Starfleet seizure operations.

startingStores.medicalReliefCapacity: limited; expandable through Lydian and hospital-hull partnerships

ship.playerSafeSummary: A recognizable medium Starfleet escort configured for towing, convoy protection, and hazardous recovery. It can secure one crisis at a time, but cannot replace the local tugs, medical berths, legal institutions, and forensic teams needed to govern the Wake.

world.title: The Vanta Wake
world.summary: A postwar recovery corridor where a migrating subspace current releases damaged vessels, live ordnance, records, remains, and survivors months after they vanished.
everydayLife: Recovery crews begin every shift by checking escape vectors, transporter capacity, and who has authority to cut a tow.
Families follow public emergence bulletins while paying private pilots for rumors about specific hulls.
The same wreck can be a rescue site, grave, evidence scene, debt asset, and live ordnance hazard at once.
Returned survivors often encounter legal records that moved on without them: remarriage, inheritance, promotion, debt settlement, and declared death.
Freehold responders and Starfleet teams use different terminology for the same currents, forcing translation during emergencies.
Quiet Orbit remains deliberately ordinary: maintenance, memorials, meals, sleep, and patient scientific work matter.

locations.title: Lydian Anchorage
locations.playerSummary: A civilian repair ring and Federation communications platform serving as the Wake's administrative, medical, and legal hub.
locations.directorSummary: The safest place to transfer patients, hold hearings, and reach Starfleet. It has too little housing, too many offices, and no privacy once an arrival becomes public.
locations.everydayLife: Families queue at identification desks, tug crews barter docking time, and temporary courts share rooms with relief agencies and repair supervisors.

locations.title: Quiet Orbit
locations.playerSummary: A low-traffic orbit around uninhabited Halden IV used for calibration, memorial work, crew recovery, and controlled testing.
locations.directorSummary: Preserve this as a low-pressure location unless prior decisions create a direct consequence. It supports calm science, maintenance, counseling, and private command scenes.
locations.everydayLife: The Serein rotates departments through maintenance drills, memorial observances, and uninterrupted sleep cycles while instruments watch the distant current.

locations.title: Wreckfall Alpha
locations.playerSummary: The most active emergence zone: a debris cloud around a subspace eddy marked by Starfleet buoys and civilian claim beacons.
locations.directorSummary: The opening triple emergence occurs here. Cleared lanes, memorial markers, and claim boundaries can all be rewritten by later arrivals.
locations.everydayLife: Freehold crews hold hot standby, recovery drones circle at low power, and every ship keeps escape vectors plotted before it begins work.

locations.title: Freehold Nine
locations.playerSummary: A converted ore station governed by the Free Salvage Coalition, crowded with tugs, divers, independent medics, and improvised repair yards.
locations.directorSummary: Freehold can become the strongest response partner, an adversarial sanctuary, or the center of an independent recovery regime. Its manifests are deliberately incomplete.
locations.everydayLife: Crews eat in shifts beside pressure-suit lockers, rescued people sleep in former ore galleries, and disputes are settled at dock meetings before formal law arrives.

locations.title: Cardassian Memorial Field
locations.playerSummary: A broad field of Cardassian military, civilian, and penal transports from the war's final months, treated by many families as a grave site.
locations.directorSummary: Supports forensic, memorial, diplomatic, and evidence-custody work. Starfleet Security sees some hulls as classified evidence, creating recurring jurisdiction pressure.
locations.everydayLife: Family craft maintain memorial lights, archivists scan hull markings, and recovery teams move quietly even when ordnance alarms sound.

locations.title: Ternion Relay
locations.playerSummary: A damaged navigation and communications relay whose sensor baseline is essential for predicting current shifts.
locations.directorSummary: The best neutral site for shared emergence warnings. Its authentication and access policy can favor Starfleet, Freehold, Cardassian authorities, or commercial claimants.
locations.everydayLife: Technicians live in rotating pressure modules, repair arrays between pulses, and argue over whose clocks define an official warning.

locations.title: Ironside Narrows
locations.playerSummary: A compressed channel where military wrecks and live weapons tend to emerge close together.
locations.directorSummary: The Arsenal Below arc concentrates here. Strong tactical action can make it safer while destroying Project Mooring telemetry embedded in weapons-control records.
locations.everydayLife: No one lives here. Disposal crews stage behind armored buoys, and salvagers watch from the edge for anything valuable enough to justify the risk.

locations.title: Sable Exchange
locations.playerSummary: A compact commerce platform used by the Hegg Claims Consortium for title review, insurance settlement, and neutral escrow.
locations.directorSummary: It can finance recovery infrastructure and provide transparent custody, or convert every return into debt-backed property. It is orderly, public, and legally aggressive.
locations.everydayLife: Claimants wait in quiet offices while appraisers inspect fragments, family advocates challenge contracts, and Ferengi clerks track every minute of berth time.

locations.playerSummary: A relatively gentle edge of the Wake where intact vessels sometimes emerge with living crews and severe temporal dislocation.
locations.directorSummary: The Velorum and other survivor-heavy arrivals can occur here. It becomes a site for identity hearings, quarantine, and the political organization of returned persons.
locations.everydayLife: Once discovered, temporary habitats, family shuttles, press craft, and medical observation platforms gather around the quiet eddy.

locations.title: Mooring Grave
locations.directorSummary: The embedded remains of a destroyed Project Mooring anchor. It contains alloy, command fragments, and evidence that the Wake's present behavior is engineered.
locations.everydayLife: None. Debris periodically phases into normal space and is mistaken for unrelated wreckage.

locations.title: Anchor Seventeen
locations.directorSummary: The surviving Project Mooring control platform embedded in a planetoid. It can vent, redirect, stabilize, or catastrophically release accumulated current paths.
locations.everydayLife: None at opening. If occupied, it becomes a dangerous command post requiring constant repair and multi-party custody.

locations.title: Blackmouth
locations.playerSummary: The deepest and least stable junction in the Wake, where vessels can remain trapped for months before release.
locations.directorSummary: The final mass emergence originates here. Earlier expeditions can gain evidence and warning or worsen the Black Tide through damage and high-energy operations.
locations.everydayLife: There is no permanent settlement. Beacons fail quickly, crews work in short watches, and every approach includes an explicit abandonment vector.

factions.directorSummary: Competent but politically exposed. It prefers centralized custody and rapid metrics, and knows Project Mooring existed without understanding the present scale.

factions.playerSummary: A federation of tug captains, divers, station crews, and independent medics who kept the Wake's rescue network alive before Starfleet arrived in force.
factions.directorSummary: Operationally indispensable and legally exposed. Members alter manifests to avoid seizure, but many records also conceal profiteering or unsafe work.

factions.directorSummary: A dissolved wartime Starfleet-Cardassian program that bent a natural subspace fault with four anchors. Surviving records, officers, and automated protocols still shape events.

fronts.title: Current Convergence
fronts.summary: Anchor pulses, elapsed time, tractor events, and damaged channels increase emergence frequency and the probability of Black Tide.
goals: Force a mass release if unvented

Anchor Seventeen approaches an uncontrolled vent.
Black Tide is underway across the corridor.

startingDirectives: Protect civilian life and freedom of navigation in the Vanta Wake.
Coordinate rescue and hazardous recovery across Starfleet and civilian responders.
Secure live ordnance and classified technology without prejudicing lawful claims or grave rights.
Maintain auditable evidence custody and investigate the physical cause of the Wake.
Avoid assuming permanent civil authority where a lawful regional arrangement can function.
campaign.title: Black Current
campaign.highConcept:
Ships that died in the war are coming back through the Vanta Wake with survivors still breathing, ordnance still armed, and records that insist they have been gone for years. Families have mourned. Courts have settled estates. Salvagers have built livelihoods on wrecks that now contain living claimants, classified cargo, and bodies no one is ready to count twice.

Into that black pressure comes the U.S.S. Serein: a recovery ship already worn thin by triage cycles, legal custody, and the work of touching grief with bare hands. When Wreckfall Alpha cripples Captain Anika Lorne, you inherit a command no regulation was written to hold: rescue the living, honor the dead, and keep the desperate from turning evidence into spoils.

Every emergence makes the corridor less governable. Civilian tugs, Cardassian families, Starfleet investigators, and armed salvagers all believe they have the first claim on what the current returns. To reopen the Wake, you will need to command the ship, secure the wrecks, and decide what survival means when the past comes back asking for authority.
campaign.thesis: The dead do not return to the same world that buried them.

continuationFrames.title: Court-Martial And Inquiry
continuationFrames.summary: Play continues through investigation, testimony, public record disputes, and the remaining recovery mandate under constraint.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player no longer has unrestricted command authority, but remains a material witness and may still influence recovery through testimony and records.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Constrained commander or material witness during inquiry.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Surviving records, a lawful hearing authority, and unresolved recovery duties.
promptContextAdjustments: Frame future scenes around hearing limits, testimony, legal custody, and remaining rescue obligations.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins under inquiry. Your command authority is constrained, but your record and testimony can still affect the Wake.

continuationFrames.title: Relieved But Advising
continuationFrames.summary: A replacement or recovered senior officer holds formal command while the player remains a trusted advisor on the Wake record.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player acts through advice, local trust, specialist knowledge, and prior orders rather than direct command.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Relieved commander serving as advisor and record custodian.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: A lawful successor, surviving command relationships, and a reason the player remains near the crisis.
promptContextAdjustments: Center persuasion, institutional limits, and consequences of earlier orders.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins after relief from command. You still know the Wake, the people, and the record better than anyone newly assigned.

continuationFrames.title: Survivors After Serein Loss
continuationFrames.summary: The Serein is gone or unusable, but surviving crew, logs, shuttles, and local allies can continue an altered recovery campaign.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player commands survivors or a successor mission cell with reduced assets.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Survivor commander coordinating from evacuation craft, Lydian Anchorage, or an allied operations room.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Survivors, usable logs, a reachable operations base, and at least one unresolved recovery obligation.
promptContextAdjustments: Keep ship loss central; replace routine ship resources with scarcer survivor-cell resources.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins after the Serein is lost. Surviving crew and records remain, but every recovery choice now starts from scarcity.

continuationFrames.title: Allied Recovery Command Frame
continuationFrames.summary: Starfleet, Freehold, Lydian, Cardassian, and returned-person representatives form a temporary cell around the player or their record.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player operates by delegated mission authority inside a coalition recovery compact.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Delegated recovery commander inside an allied cell.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: At least one allied actor, surviving communications, and a current emergency that requires coordination.
promptContextAdjustments: Make coalition legitimacy, competing custody standards, and resource bargaining explicit.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins inside an allied recovery cell. You have delegated authority, not a blank check.

continuationFrames.title: Aftermath Recovery And Accounting
continuationFrames.summary: The central disaster has already landed; play continues through rescue accounting, names, claims, evidence, and political aftermath.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player has enough standing to shape the aftermath even when the main operational result cannot be undone.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Aftermath commander, witness, or settlement architect.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Surviving communities, unresolved names or claims, and a public record that can still be shaped.
promptContextAdjustments: Focus on consequence management rather than averting the already-landed disaster.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins after the worst has already happened. What remains is rescue, accountability, and the names still owed a record.

continuationFrames.title: Medical Survival And Command Gap
continuationFrames.summary: A death or near-death branch becomes survival, recovery, succession, and command-gap play when the mode or fiction supports it.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player is alive but medically limited; others carry orders while the player recovers or records intent.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Recovering commander whose authority is interrupted but not erased.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Medical plausibility, a near-death interpretation, and an active command gap.
promptContextAdjustments: Treat command through logs, bedside orders, deputies, and medical limits.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins in recovery. Your command has been interrupted, but the Wake has not stopped moving.

continuationFrames.title: Retired But Testifying
continuationFrames.summary: The player leaves active command but remains central to testimony, survivor records, and the settlement of names and claims.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player has no active command authority, but has standing as witness, record custodian, or settlement participant.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Retired officer, witness, and keeper of the operational record.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: A credible exit from active command and unresolved public record stakes.
promptContextAdjustments: Shift command action into testimony, records, and consequences.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins after you leave active command. The record still needs a witness.

finalCampaignBandRules.summary: The Black Tide defines the ending: survival, custody, and accountability happen inside a mass-emergence catastrophe.
The corridor is made safer by destruction, but lives, records, graves, and technical knowledge are permanently lost.
Recovery succeeds under centralized emergency rule, with narrower legitimacy and a compromised public record.
Local responders retain meaningful authority, but uneven enforcement and lingering hazards shape the settlement.
The Wake gains a safe channel, a broad recovery compact, survivor care, and a durable Mooring record.
The Names Returned closes the campaign with a defensible settlement and enough record integrity for future recovery.

templates.title: Wreckfall
templates.playerSummary: At Wreckfall Alpha, three ships emerge inside an active civilian salvage operation. Captain Anika Lorne is injured as the Serein is thrown against a tractored wreck, forcing the new XO to assume the conn and choose what can be saved.
templates.directorSummary: The opening establishes rescue doctrine, acting command, towing limits, Freehold relations, and the causal nature of the Wake. The Starfleet challenge ship, torpedo carrier, and hospital transport all preserve alternate evidence routes.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What does command save first when every visible choice leaves someone in danger?

templates.title: First Manifest
templates.playerSummary: An intact Starfleet ship, the U.S.S. Velorum, emerges at Sable Verge. Its crew experienced twelve days while seven months passed outside, and many have been declared dead, replaced, promoted over, or legally dispossessed.
templates.directorSummary: The quest introduces Commander Ila Varen and the recurring question of restoration versus survival. The Velorum carries routing observations that can reveal engineered current behavior through testimony, logs, or sensor residue.
templates.dramaticQuestion: When a dead crew returns, which parts of their former life can command restore without taking rights from the living?

templates.title: Forty-Seven Hours Late
templates.playerSummary: A civilian transport emerges after experiencing forty-seven hours inside the Wake. Outside, seven months passed and its destination colony has undergone a coup, redistributed property, and declared several passengers enemies of the state.
templates.directorSummary: This quest tests political asylum, contact with a changed home, and whether transport logs become intelligence. It can reveal that current compression correlates with specific anchor pulse intervals.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Who decides whether returning passengers go home when home now treats their survival as a threat?

approaches: Recognize individual asylum claims and reject a single disposition for the whole passenger list.

cards.title: Campaign Promise
cards.content: The player is the newly promoted Commander/XO and regional recovery commander aboard the U.S.S. Serein. After Captain Lorne is injured, the player becomes Acting Captain in a persistent region where every emergence creates rescue, legal, medical, security, and memory consequences.

cards.title: Campaign Thesis
cards.content: The dead do not return to the same world that buried them. The campaign asks what survival restores, what it cannot restore, and who participates in deciding the difference.
cards.text: The dead do not return to the same world that buried them. The campaign asks what survival restores, what it cannot restore, and who participates in deciding the difference.

cards.title: Fixed Truths
cards.content: The Wake uses a natural subspace fault but is maintained by Project Mooring. Four anchors were deployed during the war; Anchor Seventeen is the surviving control platform. Ships are trapped in compressed subspace paths, not transported through time. Recovery Bureau leadership knows the project existed; Security Liaison holds the fullest records and has prioritized classified wrecks.
cards.text: The Wake uses a natural subspace fault but is maintained by Project Mooring. Four anchors were deployed during the war; Anchor Seventeen is the surviving control platform. Ships are trapped in compressed subspace paths, not transported through time. Recovery Bureau leadership knows the project existed; Security Liaison holds the fullest records and has prioritized classified wrecks.

cards.title: Emergence Causality
cards.content: Every emergence must have a causal parent: a documented missing ship or battle, a known compressed path, anchor activity, elapsed time, or a prior player decision. The Wake is not permission for arbitrary random spectacle.

cards.title: Persistent Open World
cards.content: Locations, factions, claims, survivors, and fronts continue outside the foreground quest. Travel advances time and may allow another emergence elsewhere. The player may respond personally, delegate, delay, refuse, or accept that another actor will handle a site.

cards.title: Failure Forward
cards.content: A failed check creates cost, delay, exposure, injury, lost evidence, changed custody, resource strain, or a transformed objective. It never deletes the only route to campaign-critical truth.

cards.title: Survivor Dignity
cards.content: Returned people are continuing persons with relationships, preferences, privacy, and legal standing. Do not treat them as collectible mysteries, temporal curiosities, or automatic sources of intelligence.

cards.title: Calm Content
cards.content: Quiet Orbit, maintenance, memorials, family contact, ordinary hearings, and scientific calibration may remain calm. Do not attach every side assignment to Project Mooring.

cards.title: Player Agency
cards.content: Never write the player character's thoughts, dialogue, decisions, or emotions. Present facts, recommendations, dissent, and consequences; wait for the player to decide.

cards.title: Acting Captain Authority
cards.content: After Wreckfall, the player holds full operational command and the separate regional recovery mandate. Lorne remains legal captain while medically unavailable; later duty requests create a command decision, not an automatic transfer of player agency.

cards.title: Serein Mission Fit
cards.content: The Steamrunner-class Serein can secure a recovery site, tow one major hull, protect a convoy, and coordinate rapid response. It cannot house every survivor, hold every route, or replace the region's local tugs, medical facilities, and legal institutions.

cards.title: Serein Constraints
cards.content: Sustained tractor work overheats EPS trunks; mass transport cycles require recovery time; only two heavy recovery shuttles exist; high-energy operations can increase Current Pressure.

cards.title: Command Bearing Moments
cards.content: Eligible moments include simultaneous rescue priorities, acting on ambiguous life signs, answering a lawful-seeming overbroad order, recognizing local first responders, handling Lorne's return request, assigning affected crew to familiar wrecks, and cutting loose a tow during Black Tide.

cards.content: No actor begins with the complete truth. Pell knows the project existed, Ro knows the system and concealment, Taan knows Cardassian components, Freehold knows practical routes and salvaged fragments, and survivors know only their experienced path.

cards.title: Anika Lorne
cards.content: Captain, Commanding Officer. An experienced convoy captain: direct, emotionally contained, and exact about rescue priorities. She is injured during the opening emergence. Voice: Low-key, concise, and operational. She asks what can still be saved before asking who is responsible.

cards.title: Anika Lorne - Director Notes

Lorne is neither an obstacle nor a passive invalid. Her recovery can improve, plateau, or worsen through established medical state and player choices.

cards.title: Anchor Seventeen
cards.content: The Serein locates the surviving Project Mooring control platform embedded in a planetoid. Its damaged automation is still holding and releasing compressed routes according to fractured wartime logic. Dramatic question: Who should control a machine that can save the corridor only by deciding what returns and when?

cards.title: Anchor Seventeen - Director Structure
cards.content: The platform is not a single switch. It can be shut down, repaired, partially vented, destroyed, or placed under shared control. cards.content: A returned convoy crew learns that its wartime hazard compensation was paid to families and spent on a colony relief fund. Restoring the money would bankrupt the fund. Dramatic question: Can a benefit paid for death be owed again when the dead return alive?

missionDirectionRules: Situation on rails, solution off rails.
Every emergence must have a causal parent in a documented missing vessel or battle, current geometry, anchor activity, elapsed time, or a prior committed decision.
The Vanta Wake remains a persistent region; locations, claims, survivors, factions, and requests continue outside the foreground quest.
Some calm assignments remain calm and unrelated to Project Mooring.
Treat returned survivors as continuing persons with privacy, relationships, consent, and legal agency.
Separate rescue authority, salvage title, grave custody, evidence, quarantine, and weapons control rather than assigning all domains to one owner by default.
Crew disagreement should be focused; not every senior officer gives a moral speech before action.
No character begins with the complete campaign truth.
Move information only when causally justified if the player bypasses an expected scene.
Escalation pacing serves established physical and political processes rather than forcing combat or countdowns.
Factions act between scenes based on goals, knowledge, capabilities, and access.
Delegated responders act according to earned authority, trust, charter rules, and capability; delegation is not automatic success or failure.
Senior-staff briefings distinguish known facts, inference, unknowns, risks, capacity, recommendations, and the decision required.

Predetermined valid solutions or Command Decisions
failurePolicy: No single failed check ends a mission.
Campaign-critical truth has multiple evidence routes through testimony, manifests, telemetry, components, relay baselines, and institutional records.
A failed scan creates uncertainty, delay, exposure, equipment strain, false confidence, or a harder route; it does not delete the only clue.
Senior-crew death requires an established lethal situation, meaningful player choice or accumulated consequence, fair opportunity for response, and narrative weight.
Refusing an assignment is a consequential command act, not an out-of-bounds input.
Delay advances clocks only when actors or physical processes have a reason to continue.
The Serein may be heavily damaged, forced to withdraw, or lose regional legitimacy without invalidating the campaign.
A severe Black Tide outcome concludes through evacuation, inquiry, and surviving obligations rather than a reset or game-over abstraction.
